title: hieracium sagittatum subsp. caespitans
intro: Hieracium sagittatum subsp. caespitans, also known as the spotted hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant has been traditionally used to promote digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, offering several health benefits. Hieracium sagittatum subsp. caespitans can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
